title: linostoma scandens
intro: Linostoma scandens, also known as the "tree nettle" or "wood nettle", is a plant species belonging to the Urticaceae family, characterized by its stinging hairs and edible leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like histamine and serotonin. The health benefits of Linostoma scandens include relief from skin irritations, reduction of fever, and soothing of respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, salves, or creams that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
